name: solr
explanation: Solr is collected via the JMXFetch helper (`template: instances/jmx`). Production Solr deployments enable JMX Remote with authentication and frequently TLS, requiring `user`, `password`, and key/trust-store paths. The example port (9999) is not a true convention — JMX listening ports are deployment-specific. Even a host+port probe cannot validate the JMX endpoint without credentials. Per the procedure's pattern I (JMXFetch always needs host+port+user+password), Solr falls into the impossible bucket.
notes: JMX-based integration; the example port 9999 in spec.yaml is a placeholder, not a real Solr default. Auth and TLS-for-RMI are essentially mandatory in production.
